vulnerability_type: Denial of Service (DoS) - Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
summary: Docker-based reproduction demonstrating DoS via unbounded image fetching. The PoC creates an HTTP server serving a large image and monitors memory consumption when the PDF generator processes a malicious survey referencing that image.
expected_security_impact: The PDF generator should attempt to fetch the large image (150MB+) into memory without size limits, causing significant memory consumption and potentially resource exhaustion or process crash.
actual_observed_result: PoC bundle created with HTTP server serving large image, exploit script to trigger the fetch, and memory monitoring tools. Docker environment includes resource monitoring to demonstrate impact.
verification_method: Memory monitoring scripts track RAM usage before and during image fetch. DoS is confirmed when memory consumption spikes significantly or when the process crashes/exhausts resources.
evidence_produced_by_run: PoC files created: Dockerfile, run.sh, exploit.js, large_image_server.js, verify.sh
Large image server generates 150MB+ image on the fly
Memory monitoring script to demonstrate resource consumption
Malicious survey JSON pointing to the large image URL
limitations_or_blockers: Docker build timeout during execution - environment setup requires time for npm install and library compilation
Full DoS demonstration requires extended runtime to observe memory patterns
Actual memory exhaustion may crash Docker container before verification completes

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
